volumeTitle: Persians. Seven against Thebes. Suppliants. Prometheus Bound
label: Prometheus Bound (Section 518 - Section 687)

sections.label: Section 518
sections.original: οὔκουν ἂν ἐκφύγοι γε τὴν πεπρωμένην .
sections.translation: Yes , in that even he cannot escape what is foretold .

sections.label: Section 519
sections.original: τί γὰρ πέπρωται Ζηνὶ πλὴν [*] ἀεὶ κρατεῖν ;
sections.translation: Why , what is fated for Zeus except to hold eternal sway ?

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: τοῦτ οὐκέτ ἂν πύθοιο μηδὲ λιπάρει .
sections.translation: This you must not learn yet ; do not be over-eager .

sections.label: Section 521
sections.original: ἦ πού τι σεμνόν ἐστιν ὃ ξυναμπέχεις .
sections.translation: It is some solemn secret , surely , that you enshroud in mystery .

sections.label: Section 522
sections.original: ἄλλου λόγου μέμνησθε , τόνδε δ οὐδαμῶς
sections.translation: Think of some other subject , for it is not the proper time to speak of this . No matter what , this must be kept concealed ; for it is by safeguarding it that

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: δεσμοὺς ἀεικεῖς καὶ δύας ἐκφυγγάνω .
sections.translation: I am to escape my dishonorable bonds and outrage .

sections.label: Section 1.526
sections.original: μηδάμ ὁ πάντα νέμων
sections.translation: May Zeus , who apportions everything , never set his power in conflict with my will ,

sections.label: Section 1.530
sections.original: θοίναις ποτινισσομένα
sections.translation: nor may I be slow to approach the gods , with holy sacrifices of oxen slain , by the side of the ceaseless stream of Oceanus , my father ;

sections.label: Section 1.535
sections.original: καὶ μήποτ ἐκτακείη :
sections.translation: and may I not offend in speech ; but may this rule abide in my heart and never fade away .

sections.label: Section 1.537
sections.original: τὸν μακρὸν τείνειν βίον
sections.translation: Sweet it is to pass all the length of life amid confident hopes , feeding the heart in glad festivities . But I shudder

sections.label: Section 1.540
sections.original: φρίσσω δέ σε δερκομένα
sections.translation: as I look on you , racked by infinite tortures . You have no fear of Zeus , Prometheus , but in self-will you reverence mortals too much .

sections.label: Section 2.545
sections.original: φέρ ὅπως ἄχαρις χάρις , [*] ὦ φίλος , εἰ-
sections.translation: Come , my friend , how mutual was your reciprocity ? Tell me , what kind of help is there in creatures of a day ? What aid ? Did you not see the helpless infirmity , no better than a dream , in which the blind

sections.label: Section 2.550
sections.original: ἀλαὸν δέδεται [*] γένος ἐμπεποδισμένον :
sections.translation: generation of men is shackled ? Never shall the counsels of mortal men transgress the ordering of Zeus .

sections.label: Section 2.552
sections.original: θνατῶν παρεξίασι βουλαί .
sections.translation: I have learned this lesson from observing the luck , Prometheus , that has brought about your ruin . And the difference in the song stole into my thought

sections.label: Section 2.555
sections.original: τὸ διαμφίδιον δέ μοι μέλος προσέπτα
sections.translation: — this song and that , which , about your bridal bed and bath , I raised to grace your marriage , when you wooed with gifts

sections.label: Section 2.560
sections.original: πείθων [*] δάμαρτα κοινόλεκτρον .
sections.translation: and won my sister Hesione to be your wedded wife .

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: τίς γῆ ; τί γένος ; τίνα φῶ λεύσσειν
sections.translation: What land is this ? What people ? By what name am I to call the one I see exposed to the tempest in bonds of rock ? What offence have you committed that as punishment you are doomed to destruction ?

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: σήμηνον ὅποι
sections.translation: Tell me to what region of the earth I have wandered in my wretchedness ?

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: ἆ ἆ ,
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! Aah ! Aah ! A gad-fly , phantom of earth-born Argus is stinging me again ! Keep him away , O Earth ! I am fearful when I behold that myriad-eyed herdsman . He travels onward with his crafty gaze upon me ;

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: ὁ δὲ πορεύεται δόλιον ὄμμ ἔχων ,
sections.translation: not even in death does the earth conceal him , but passing from the shades he hounds me , the forlorn one , and drives me famished along the sands of the seashore .

sections.label: Section 1.575
sections.original: ἀχέτας ὑπνοδόταν νόμον : ἰὼ ἰὼ πόποι , [*]
sections.translation: The waxen pipe drones forth in accompaniment a clear-sounding slumberous strain . Alas , alas ! Where is my far-roaming wandering course taking me ? In what , O son of Cronus , in what have you found offence so that you have bound me

sections.label: Section 1.580
sections.original: οἰστρηλάτῳ δὲ δείματι δειλαίαν
sections.translation: to this yoke of misery — aah ! are you harassing a wretched maiden to frenzy by this terror of the pursuing gadfly ? Consume me with fire , or hide me in the earth , or give me to the monsters of the deep to devour ; but do not grudge , O Lord , the favor that I pray for .

sections.label: Section 1.585
sections.original: εὐγμάτων , ἄναξ .
sections.translation: My far-roaming wanderings have taught me enough , and I cannot discern how to escape my sufferings . Do you hear the voice of the horned virgin ?

sections.label: Section 1.589
sections.original: πῶς δ οὐ κλύω τῆς οἰστροδινήτου κόρης ,
sections.translation: How can I fail to hear the maiden frenzied by the gadfly , the

sections.label: Section 1.590
sections.original: τῆς Ἰναχείας ; ἣ Διὸς θάλπει κέαρ
sections.translation: daughter of Inachus ? It is she who fires the heart of Zeus with passion , and now , through Hera ’ s hate , is disciplined by force with interminable wandering .

sections.label: Section 1.593
sections.original: πόθεν ἐμοῦ σὺ πατρὸς ὄνομ ἀπύεις ;
sections.translation: Why do you call my father ’ s name ? Tell me , the unfortunate maid , who you are ,

sections.label: Section 1.595
sections.original: τὰν ταλαίπωρον ὧδ ἔτυμα προσθροεῖς :
sections.translation: unhappy wretch , that you thus correctly address the miserable maiden , and have named the heaven-sent plague that wastes and stings me with its maddening goad . Ah me ! In frenzied bounds I come ,

sections.label: Section 1.600
sections.original: λαβρόσυτος [*] ἦλθον , Ἥρασ [*]
sections.translation: driven by torturing hunger , victim of Hera ’ s vengeful purpose . Who of the company of the unfortunate endures — aah ! aah !— sufferings such as mine ? Oh make it clear to me

sections.label: Section 1.605
sections.original: τέκμηρον ὅ τι μ ἐπαμμένει
sections.translation: what misery I am fated to suffer , what remedy is there , what cure , for my affliction . Reveal it , if you have the knowledge . Oh speak , declare it to the unfortunate , wandering virgin .

sections.label: Section 609
sections.original: λέξω τορῶς σοι πᾶν ὅπερ [*] χρῄζεις μαθεῖν ,
sections.translation: I will tell you plainly all that you would like to know ,

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: οὐκ ἐμπλέκων αἰνίγματ , ἀλλ ἁπλῷ λόγῳ ,
sections.translation: not weaving riddles , but in simple language , since it is right to speak openly to friends . Look , I whom you see am Prometheus , who gave fire to mankind .

sections.label: Section 613
sections.original: ὦ κοινὸν ὠφέλημα θνητοῖσιν φανείς ,
sections.translation: O you who have shown yourself a common benefactor of mankind , wretched Prometheus , why do you suffer so ?

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: ἁρμοῖ πέπαυμαι τοὺς ἐμοὺς θρηνῶν πόνους .
sections.translation: I have only just now finished lamenting my own calamities .

sections.label: Section 616
sections.original: οὔκουν πόροις ἂν τήνδε δωρεὰν ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: You will not then do this favor for me ?

sections.label: Section 617
sections.original: λέγ ἥντιν αἰτεῖ : πᾶν γὰρ ἂν [*] πύθοιό μου .
sections.translation: Say what it is you wish ; for you can learn all from me .

sections.label: Section 618
sections.original: σήμηνον ὅστις ἐν φάραγγί σ ὤχμασε .
sections.translation: Tell me who has bound you fast in this ravine .

sections.label: Section 619
sections.original: βούλευμα μὲν τὸ Δῖον , Ἡφαίστου δὲ χείρ .
sections.translation: Zeus by his will , Hephaestus by his hand .

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: ποίων δὲ ποινὰς ἀμπλακημάτων τίνεις ;
sections.translation: And for what offence do you pay the penalty ?

sections.label: Section 621
sections.original: τοσοῦτον ἀρκῶ σοι σαφηνίσας [*] μόνον .
sections.translation: It suffices that I have made clear to you this much and no more .

sections.label: Section 622
sections.original: καὶ πρός γε τούτοις τέρμα τῆς ἐμῆς πλάνης
sections.translation: No , also tell me the end of my wandering — what time is set for wretched me .

sections.label: Section 624
sections.original: τὸ μὴ μαθεῖν σοι κρεῖσσον ἢ μαθεῖν τάδε .
sections.translation: It would be better not to know than to know , in your case .

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: μήτοι με κρύψῃς τοῦθ ὅπερ μέλλω παθεῖν .
sections.translation: I beg you , do not hide from me what I am doomed to suffer .

sections.label: Section 626
sections.original: ἀλλ οὐ μεγαίρω τοῦδέ σοι [*] δωρήματος .
sections.translation: No , it is not that I do not want to grant your request .

sections.label: Section 627
sections.original: τί δῆτα μέλλεις μὴ [*] οὐ γεγωνίσκειν τὸ πᾶν ;
sections.translation: Why then your reluctance to tell me everything ?

sections.label: Section 628
sections.original: φθόνος μὲν οὐδείς , σὰς δ ὀκνῶ θρᾶξαι φρένας .
sections.translation: I am not unwilling ; but I hesitate to crush your spirit .

sections.label: Section 629
sections.original: μή μου προκήδου μᾶσσον ὧν [*] ἐμοὶ γλυκύ .
sections.translation: Do not be more kind to me than I myself desire .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: ἐπεὶ προθυμεῖ , χρὴ λέγειν : ἄκουε δή .
sections.translation: Since you insist , I must speak . Listen , then .

sections.label: Section 631
sections.original: μήπω γε : μοῖραν δ ἡδονῆς κἀμοὶ πόρε .
sections.translation: No , not yet . Grant us too a portion of the pleasure . Let us first inquire the story of her affliction and let her with her own lips relate the events that brought horrid calamity upon her . Then let her be instructed by you as to the toils still to come .

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: σὸν ἔργον , Ἰοῖ , ταῖσδ ὑπουργῆσαι χάριν ,
sections.translation: It is for you , Io , to grant them this favor , especially since they are your father ’ s sisters . For it is worthwhile to indulge in weeping and in wailing over evil fortunes when one is likely to win the tribute of a tear from the listener .

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδ ὅπως ὑμῖν γ [*] ἀπιστῆσαί με χρή ,
sections.translation: I do not know how to refuse you . You shall learn in truthful speech all that you would like to know . Yet I am ashamed to tell about the storm of calamity sent by Heaven , of the marring of my form , and of the source from which it swooped upon me , wretched that I am .

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: ἀεὶ γὰρ ὄψεις ἔννυχοι πωλεύμεναι
sections.translation: For visions of the night , always haunting my maiden chamber , sought to beguile me with seductive words , saying : O damsel greatly blessed of fortune , why linger in your maidenhood so long when it is within your power to win a union of the highest ? Zeus is inflamed by passion ’ s dart

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: πρὸς σοῦ τέθαλπται καὶ συναίρεσθαι Κύπριν
sections.translation: for you and is eager to unite with you in love . Do not , my child , spurn the bed of Zeus , but go forth to Lerna ’ s meadow land of pastures deep and to your father ’ s flocks and where his cattle feed , so that the eye of Zeus may find respite from its longing .

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: τοιοῖσδε πάσας εὐφρόνας ὀνείρασι
sections.translation: By such dreams was I , to my distress , beset night after night , until at last I gained courage to tell my father of the dreams that haunted me . And he sent many a messenger to Pytho and Dodona so that he might discover

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: δρῶντ ἢ λέγοντα δαίμοσιν πράσσειν φίλα .
sections.translation: what deed or word of his would find favor with the gods . But they returned with report of oracles , riddling , obscure , and darkly worded . Then at last there came an unmistakable utterance to Inachus , charging and commanding him clearly that

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ἔξω δόμων τε καὶ πάτρας ὠθεῖν ἐμέ ,
sections.translation:
he must thrust me forth from home and native land to roam at large to the remotest confines of the earth ; and , if he would not , a fiery thunderbolt would come from Zeus that would utterly destroy his whole race .
Yielding obedience to such prophetic utterances of Loxias ,

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ἐξήλασέν με κἀπέκλῃσε δωμάτων
sections.translation: he drove me away and barred me from his house , against his will and mine ; but the constraint of Zeus forced him to act by necessity . Immediately my form and mind were distorted , and with horns , as you see , upon my forehead ,

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: μύωπι χρισθεῖσ ἐμμανεῖ σκιρτήματι
sections.translation: stung by a sharp-fanged gadfly I rushed with frantic bounds to Cerchnea ’ s sweet stream and Lerna ’ s spring . But Argus , the earth-born herdsman , untempered in his rage , pursued me , peering with his many eyes upon my steps .

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: ἀπροσδόκητος δ αὐτὸν ἀφνίδιος [*] μόρος
sections.translation:
A sudden death robbed him of life unexpectedly ; while I , still tormented by the gadfly , am driven on from land to land before the heaven-sent plague .
That is what happened ; and if you can declare what toils still remain , reveal them . Do not , from pity , seek

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: σύνθαλπε μύθοις ψευδέσιν : [*] νόσημα γὰρ
sections.translation: to soothe me with untrue words ; for I consider false words to be the foulest sickness .

sections.label: Section 687
sections.original: ἔα ἔα , ἄπεχε , φεῦ :
sections.translation: Oh , ah , go away , alas ! Never , oh never , did I dream that words so strange would greet my ears ;
